channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: NASA and Red Hat are building an open source medical system to diagnose sick astronauts on the ISS — could a Star Trek Tricorder be next?
item.description: NASA and Red Hat are testing an offline AI medical assistant called CMO-DA to help astronauts diagnose and treat conditions during deep space missions.

item.title: 'We can tune the bass to get more output, pretty much for free': I asked audio specialist Audeze about its patented SLAM system for headphones, and the slam-dunks kept a-coming
item.description: Audeze's SLAM tech was a breakthrough for the brand — but what exactly is it and how did it come to be? I asked the people who created it

item.title: ErgoDriven Tempo Automatic Standing Desk review: An impressively well-built desk that actively encourages you to sit and stand
item.description: The ErgoDriven Tempo standing desk becomes an active part of your workday, encouraging you to stand up by actually standing you up.

item.title: Rocket Lab buys Iridium in an $8 billion deal to build the most formidable rival to Musk's SpaceX — will it change the Ukraine war?
item.description: Rocket Lab's $8 billion Iridium acquisition creates a vertically integrated satellite company spanning launch, spacecraft and communications networks.

item.title: The Space Shuttle Endeavour goes on public display later this year
item.description: The spacecraft has been in LA since 2012, but now it's moving to a new permanent home at the California Science Center.

item.title: How to watch the 2026 Tour de France online for free
item.description: How to watch the Tour de France for free. Live stream the 2026 Tour de France for free from anywhere in the world.

item.title: Scientists say Elon Musk’s orbital data centers could blind Earth’s biggest telescopes
item.description: Scientists are urging limits on satellite launches after simulations showed some observatories could become effectively unusable for hours every night. If Elon Musk has his way, stargazing could soon be impossible.
